gi_source.title: Glycemic index not applicable — negligible-carbohydrate beverage; reference: University of Sydney GI Database methodology
gi_source.source_match_notes: Flavoured green tea with no sugar contains 0 g carbohydrate per 100 g as confirmed by HPB Singapore (cr_id D09020026). Per University of Sydney GI Database methodology, a GI value cannot be meaningfully assigned to foods with negligible available carbohydrate. GI is recorded as 0 (numeric sentinel for not-applicable in this context) with gi_category set to not_applicable. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 by calculation (GL = GI x carbohydrate_g / 100; carbohydrate_g = 0).

hypertension.key_factor: Zero sodium and zero sugar; green tea catechins (EGCG) have evidence-based vasodilatory effects
hypertension.guidance: Flavoured green tea with no sugar is suitable for individuals with hypertension; it contains 0 mg sodium and 0 g sugar per 100 g, and green tea EGCG catechins have demonstrated endothelial-protective and mild vasodilatory effects in clinical studies, though caffeine content may cause transient blood pressure elevation if consumed in excess of 4–5 cups per day.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrate and zero sugar; no glycaemic impact; EGCG catechins have insulin-sensitising properties
type2_diabetes.guidance: Flavoured green tea with no sugar is appropriate for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; it contains 0 g carbohydrate and 0 g sugar per 100 g, producing no glycaemic response, and green tea EGCG catechins have been associated with improved insulin sensitivity in peer-reviewed studies; consumers should verify that the flavouring agent does not introduce hidden sugars by checking the product ingredient label.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero fat, zero saturated fat, zero sodium, zero sugar; green tea polyphenols associated with cardioprotective effects
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Flavoured green tea with no sugar is suitable for individuals with cardiovascular disease; it contains 0 g fat, 0 g saturated fat, 0 mg sodium, and 0 g sugar per 100 g, and regular consumption of green tea polyphenols (EGCG) has been associated with reduced LDL oxidation and improved endothelial function in epidemiological and intervention studies.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Green tea EGCG catechins and polyphenols with anti-inflammatory properties; zero pro-inflammatory macronutrients
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Flavoured green tea with no sugar is suitable for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis; green tea is a rich source of EGCG catechins and polyphenols with well-documented anti-inflammatory properties, and the absence of sugar, fat, and sodium means no pro-inflammatory dietary factors are present; consumers should verify that artificial flavourings do not include nightshade-derived compounds such as chili or pepper flavouring which may trigger symptoms in sensitive individuals.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Flavoured green tea (no sugar, any flavour) is a ready-to-drink or freshly brewed beverage made from green tea leaves (Camellia sinensis) that have been infused with natural or artificial flavourings — such as jasmine, lemon, peach, mint, lychee, or other fruit and floral variants — and contain no added sugar whatsoever. The production process typically involves steeping or cold-brewing green tea leaves at controlled temperatures to preserve the characteristic light, grassy, and slightly astringent flavour of green tea, after which flavouring agents are introduced to create aromatic complexity without contributing calories, carbohydrates, or sugar. The resulting beverage is clear to pale golden-green in colour, with a delicate floral or fruity aroma layered over the subtle vegetal base note of green tea; the mouthfeel is thin and refreshing, similar to lightly flavoured water. Per 100 g, this beverage provides 0 kcal energy, 0 g protein, 0 g fat, 0 g saturated fat, 0 g carbohydrate, 0 g sugar, 0 g dietary fibre, and 0 mg sodium, making it an essentially calorie-free and macro-free hydration option suitable for virtually all dietary patterns including ketogenic, low-calorie, and low-sodium diets. Green tea is well recognised in nutritional science for its high content of epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG) and other catechin polyphenols, which have been extensively studied for their antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, insulin-sensitising, and cardioprotective properties. Clinically, this beverage is suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes (zero glycaemic impact, EGCG-associated insulin sensitisation), hypertension (zero sodium, vasodilatory catechins), cardiovascular disease (zero saturated fat, cardioprotective polyphenols reducing LDL oxidation), and rheumatoid arthritis (anti-inflammatory EGCG and polyphenol content). It is vegan, vegetarian, gluten-free, dairy-free, halal, and kosher. This is a generic record covering any flavour variant of unsweetened green tea; consumers should verify that the specific flavouring used in a given product does not introduce hidden sugars or sodium by checking the product ingredient label. Moderate consumption of 2–3 cups per day is generally recommended; excessive caffeine intake from green tea may cause transient blood pressure elevation or interact with certain medications including anticoagulants and some RA therapies.

Can I drink unsweetened green tea if I have high blood pressure?
How many calories are in flavoured green tea with no sugar?
What are the health benefits of unsweetened flavoured green tea?
Is flavoured green tea suitable for weight loss?
Does no-sugar green tea have any carbohydrates?
Is flavoured green tea safe for people with rheumatoid arthritis?

confidence_reason: Input record cr_id D09020026 is sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo with a stated confidence of 0.95 and review_status of reviewed. All per-100g macronutrient values are confirmed as 0.0 by HPB, which is consistent with the known nutritional profile of unsweetened flavoured green tea (essentially water with trace tea solids and flavourings). No conflicting data was found in USDA FoodData Central for comparable unsweetened green tea beverages.
notes: Zero-macro justification: protein_g is naturally zero as flavoured green tea with no sugar is an aqueous beverage with negligible dissolved solids; fat_g is naturally zero by product formulation (no fat-containing ingredients); carbohydrate_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id D09020026, confidence 0.95, review_status reviewed); energy_kcal is naturally zero as all macronutrients are zero. Saturated_fat_g, sugar_g, dietary_fibre_g, sodium_mg, calcium_mg, and iron_mg are all confirmed zero per HPB source. Negative sentinel values (-1) applied to: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g — detailed fatty acid breakdown not reported in HPB Foodo for this beverage category; potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g — micronutrient and water content data not reported in HPB Foodo for this beverage category as of 2026. Glycemic index: this food contains negligible carbohydrates (0 g per 100 g); per University of Sydney GI Database methodology a GI value cannot be meaningfully assigned; gi_category set to not_applicable and glycemic_index set to numeric 0 as a sentinel value consistent with the template numeric requirement; glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 by calculation. This is a generic record covering any flavour variant of unsweetened green tea; specific flavour variants (e.g., jasmine, lemon, peach) may have marginally different micronutrient profiles depending on the flavouring agent used. Consumers should verify the ingredient label of specific products to confirm absence of hidden sugars or sodium in the flavouring.
